name: LiftFund Houston
description_short: LiftFund provides small business loans, grants, and advisory services to entrepreneurs who cannot access traditional financing, with specialized support across Texas and New Mexico.
description_long:
LiftFund is a non-traditional small business lender focused on serving underbanked and underserved entrepreneurs across Texas and New Mexico. The organization was established to bridge the funding gap for business owners who face barriers to conventional bank financing due to limited credit history, collateral constraints, or other factors that prevent access to traditional lending paths.

The company offers multiple forms of support including direct business loans, grants, and comprehensive business advisory services. Their service model combines financial products with personalized guidance, providing borrowers not just capital but also mentorship and strategic support through their network of lending specialists and business advisors. The contact page indicates they have specialized lending experts covering distinct regional territories (Central Texas, Texas Gulf Coast, East Texas, West Texas, North Texas, and Southwest Texas/Laredo areas) plus dedicated business advisors for major metropolitan areas including Houston and Dallas-Fort Worth.

LiftFund distinguishes itself through culturally competent lending, evident from their team composition and explicit Spanish-language availability across multiple lending specialist roles. Rather than automated underwriting, they emphasize personal consultation and relationship-based lending, inviting prospective borrowers to "talk with an expert" and book direct appointments with assigned specialists. This approach suggests flexibility in underwriting criteria compared to traditional lenders.

Limitations include that the website provides minimal detail on specific loan amounts, rates, terms, or approval criteria. The scope appears regional (Texas and New Mexico only) rather than national. No information is available regarding grant eligibility, loan processing timelines, or cost structures. Prospective borrowers must contact the company directly or schedule appointments to learn specifics about their particular loan options.
pros: Specialized support for small business owners who cannot access traditional bank financing
Bilingual lending team with Spanish-language specialists throughout service regions
Combination of loans, grants, and business advisory services in one organization
Personalized consultation model with dedicated regional lending specialists and assigned business advisors
Multiple regional lending experts covering distinct Texas and New Mexico territories
Explicit focus on underserved and underbanked entrepreneur populations
Book appointment scheduling system for direct expert consultation
cons: Website provides no specifics on loan amounts, APR ranges, repayment terms, or eligibility criteria
Geographic service limited to Texas and New Mexico only—not a nationwide option
No information about grant qualification requirements or availability
No details on application timeline, funding speed, or approval rates
Minimal transparency on loan costs and fee structure
best_for: Texas and New Mexico small business owners rejected by traditional banks
Minority-owned businesses seeking culturally competent lending and bilingual support
Entrepreneurs needing business advisory services alongside capital access
diagnosis: LiftFund is best for small business owners in Texas or New Mexico who have been rejected by traditional lenders and want personalized, relationship-based lending with advisory support. The main caveat is that the website lacks transparency on loan specifics (amounts, rates, terms, approval criteria), requiring direct contact to determine actual eligibility and suitability.
services: Appointment scheduling with assigned lending specialists
Regional lending specialist support across Texas territories
Business advisor matching for Dallas-Fort Worth and Houston regions
Support for businesses unable to access traditional financing
Multi-language lending support (Spanish language specialists available)
Nationwide business advisory services (through D'Undray Peterson)
